name: BPC-157
shortDescription: A powerhouse recovery peptide that accelerates healing from injuries and supports gut health.
whatItIs: BPC-157 (Body Protection Compound) is a synthetic peptide derived from a protein found in human gastric juice. It has been studied extensively for its remarkable healing properties.
howItWorks: It promotes the formation of new blood vessels (angiogenesis), which speeds up tissue repair and reduces inflammation. It also protects and heals the gut lining.
dosing: 250–500 mcg per day, injected subcutaneously near the injury site or taken orally for gut issues.
weekByWeek: Week 1–2: Reduced soreness and inflammation
Week 3–4: Noticeable improvement in injury or gut discomfort
Week 6+: Significant tissue repair and recovery
bestFor: Athletes recovering from injuries, people with gut issues like IBS or leaky gut

name: TB-500
shortDescription: Promotes systemic tissue repair and flexibility — great for chronic injuries.
whatItIs: TB-500 is a synthetic version of Thymosin Beta-4, a naturally occurring protein in the body that plays a key role in tissue repair.
howItWorks: It promotes cell migration and proliferation, helping the body repair damaged tissue anywhere in the body — not just at the injection site.
dosing: 2–2.5 mg twice per week for the first 4–6 weeks (loading phase), then 2–2.5 mg once per week.
weekByWeek: Week 1–2: Improved flexibility and reduced stiffness
Week 3–4: Noticeable reduction in chronic pain
Week 6+: Substantial tissue repair
bestFor: People with chronic injuries or recurring pain who want systemic healing

name: Ipamorelin
shortDescription: A gentle growth hormone releaser that improves sleep quality and body composition.
whatItIs: Ipamorelin is a growth hormone secretagogue — it tells your pituitary gland to release growth hormone in a natural, pulsatile way.
howItWorks: It binds to ghrelin receptors, triggering GH release without the spikes in cortisol or appetite that other GH releasers can cause. Very clean and well-tolerated.
weekByWeek: Week 1–2: Improved sleep depth and recovery
Week 3–4: Increased energy and better body composition
Week 8+: Noticeable changes in muscle tone and fat
bestFor: People who want better sleep and gradual body composition improvements without aggressive GH therapy

name: CJC-1295
shortDescription: Amplifies growth hormone output for muscle gain and fat loss — typically stacked with Ipamorelin.
whatItIs: CJC-1295 is a GHRH (Growth Hormone Releasing Hormone) analog that extends the half-life of your body's natural GH pulses.
howItWorks: It amplifies the natural GH pulses already happening in your body, leading to sustained higher GH and IGF-1 levels over time.
dosing: 1–2 mg per week, typically split into smaller doses. Almost always combined with Ipamorelin.
weekByWeek: Week 1–2: Better sleep and recovery
Week 4–6: Noticeable muscle fullness and fat changes
Week 8+: Significant body composition improvements
bestFor: People serious about body composition who want more pronounced GH benefits than Ipamorelin alone

name: AOD-9604
shortDescription: A targeted fat-burning peptide derived from HGH — without the side effects.
whatItIs: AOD-9604 is a modified fragment of human growth hormone specifically designed to target fat metabolism.
howItWorks: It mimics the way natural growth hormone regulates fat metabolism — stimulating lipolysis (fat breakdown) and inhibiting lipogenesis (fat creation).
dosing: 300 mcg per day, injected subcutaneously in the morning on an empty stomach.
weekByWeek: Week 1–2: Increased energy and appetite regulation
Week 4–6: Noticeable reduction in stubborn fat
Week 12+: Significant fat loss results
bestFor: People struggling with stubborn fat who are already eating well and exercising

name: Semax
shortDescription: A Russian-developed nootropic peptide that sharpens focus and protects the brain.
whatItIs: Semax is a synthetic peptide derived from ACTH, originally developed in Russia for stroke recovery. Now widely used as a cognitive enhancer.
howItWorks: It increases BDNF (brain-derived neurotrophic factor), enhances dopamine and serotonin activity, and has neuroprotective effects.
dosing: 300–600 mcg per day intranasally (nasal drops). No injection required.
weekByWeek: Day 1–3: Noticeable increase in focus and mental clarity
Week 1–2: Improved mood and motivation
Week 4+: Enhanced memory and cognitive performance
bestFor: Knowledge workers, students, or anyone wanting a cognitive edge without stimulants

name: Selank
shortDescription: A calming nootropic peptide that reduces anxiety without sedation.
whatItIs: Selank is a synthetic peptide based on the immune peptide Tuftsin, developed in Russia as an anxiolytic (anti-anxiety) compound.
howItWorks: It modulates GABA receptors and increases serotonin and dopamine activity, producing a calm, focused state without sedation or dependence.
weekByWeek: Day 1: Noticeable calming effect within hours
Week 1–2: Reduced baseline anxiety and better stress response
Week 3–4: Improved overall mood and social ease
bestFor: People dealing with anxiety, high stress, or wanting calm focus without pharmaceuticals

name: DSIP
shortDescription: Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide — naturally improves deep sleep quality.
whatItIs: DSIP (Delta Sleep-Inducing Peptide) is a naturally occurring neuropeptide that promotes deep, restorative sleep.
howItWorks: It modulates delta brain wave activity — the brain state associated with deep, restorative sleep — and also has mild stress-reducing effects.
weekByWeek: Night 1–3: Noticeably deeper and more restful sleep
Week 1–2: Better morning energy and mood
Week 3–4: Improved overall recovery and stress levels
bestFor: People with poor sleep quality, insomnia, or high-stress lifestyles

name: Epithalon
shortDescription: A longevity peptide that activates telomerase and improves sleep — popular in anti-aging circles.
whatItIs: Epithalon is a tetrapeptide (4 amino acids) developed by Russian scientist Vladimir Khavinson. It's considered one of the most promising longevity compounds.
howItWorks: It activates telomerase, an enzyme that repairs and extends telomeres (the protective caps on DNA). Longer telomeres are associated with longer healthspan.
dosing: 5–10 mg per day for 10–20 days, then a long break (several months). Run 1–2 cycles per year.
weekByWeek: Days 1–5: Improved sleep quality
Days 6–10: Increased energy and wellbeing
Months later: Long-term anti-aging and cellular health benefits
bestFor: Health-conscious individuals focused on longevity and long-term cellular health

name: GHK-Cu
shortDescription: A copper peptide that visibly improves skin, hair, and collagen — popular in skincare.
whatItIs: GHK-Cu (Copper Peptide) is a naturally occurring copper complex found in human blood plasma. It's widely used in high-end skincare products.
howItWorks: It stimulates collagen and elastin production, promotes wound healing, activates antioxidant enzymes, and has anti-inflammatory properties.
dosing: Applied topically as a serum or cream, 1–2x daily. Can also be injected for more pronounced effects.
cycleLength: Ongoing use as part of skincare routine
weekByWeek: Week 1–2: Improved skin texture and hydration
Week 4–6: Visible reduction in fine lines
Week 8+: Significant collagen and skin quality improvement
bestFor: Anyone interested in skin health, anti-aging skincare, or hair improvement

name: NAD+
shortDescription: A cellular energy molecule that fuels mitochondria and supports longevity.
whatItIs: NAD+ (Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide) is a coenzyme found in every cell of the body. Levels decline with age, contributing to fatigue and aging.
howItWorks: It powers mitochondrial function (your cells' energy factories), activates sirtuins (longevity proteins), and supports DNA repair.
dosing: 250–500 mg IV or 100–300 mg subcutaneous injection. Oral NMN/NR are precursors and a cheaper alternative.
weekByWeek: Day 1: Significant energy boost (especially IV)
Week 1–2: Improved mental clarity and physical stamina
Month 1+: Long-term cellular health and energy improvements
bestFor: People experiencing fatigue, brain fog, or focused on longevity and cellular health
